title: fraxinus oxycarpa var. parvifolia
intro: Fraxinus oxycarpa var. parvifolia, also known as the Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and small white flowers. The bark and leaves of this plant are rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Fraxinus oxycarpa var. parvifolia include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve the bark and leaves being dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, including cardiovascular disease and respiratory issues.
